title: Jump Game
description: Swiggy maps a delivery street as an array `maxJump` where `maxJump[i]` is the maximum number of buildings a rider standing at index `i` can hop forward (because of fuel left). The rider starts at index 0. Return `true` if the rider can reach the last building, otherwise `false`.
hints: Track sabse aage tak ka reachable index ek pass me.
Agar current index reachable se aage hai toh wahi pe atak gaya.
Single element ka case: already at end, return true.

title: Minimum Number of Arrows to Burst Balloons
description:
Razorpay batches refunds: each refund is an interval `[start, end]` describing when it must be processed. A batch worker can hold any number of refunds whose intervals all overlap at one common instant. Return the minimum number of batch workers needed so every refund is covered. Equivalently, this is the classic 'jump game II' on an array: given `maxJump[i]`, return minimum hops from index 0 to the last index. We use the second formulation directly.

Given `maxJump`, return the minimum number of jumps to reach the last index. You may assume the last index is always reachable.
hints: Window-based BFS: current jump's reachable range is [l, r]; next range is [r+1, max(i+maxJump[i]) for i in window].
Har baar window khatam hone par jumps++ kar.
Single-element array → 0 jumps.

title: Gas Station
description: An IndianOil tanker drives a circular highway with `n` gas stations. At station `i` it can refill `gas[i]` litres, and going from station `i` to `i+1` costs `cost[i]` litres. Starting with an empty tank, return the index of the station from which you can complete a full clockwise loop. Return -1 if impossible. There is at most one valid start (when one exists).
hints: Agar total gas < total cost, answer -1.
Otherwise unique start exists. Track running tank; jab negative ho, next index se restart kar.
Sum-condition guarantee karta hai single pass kaafi hai.

title: Hand of Straights
description: Flipkart's packing line wants to ship products in bundles of exactly `groupSize`, where each bundle consists of `groupSize` consecutive product IDs (e.g., [4,5,6] for size 3). Given `productIds` and `groupSize`, return `true` if the array can be split entirely into such bundles, otherwise `false`.
hints: Sabse chhota element kisi bundle ka start hi banega — usse start kar.
TreeMap / sorted Map of frequencies use kar; iterate ascending key.
Length % groupSize !== 0 → straight false.

title: Merge Triplets to Form Target Triplet
description: Zomato rates each delivery batch by a triplet `[speed, accuracy, freshness]`. Operation: pick any two triplets `a` and `b` and replace either with the elementwise max. Given `triplets` and a target `target = [tx, ty, tz]`, decide whether some sequence of operations yields a triplet exactly equal to `target`. Return `true` or `false`.

examples.explanation: No triplet has y <= 2 and matches x=3.

hints: Koi triplet jisme koi component target se bada hai, vo useless hai (use karoge toh max barbaad).
Useful triplets ka componentwise max nikaalo.
Vo max == target ho toh true.

title: Partition Labels
description: Paytm processes a stream of cashback events represented as a string `events` where each character is a campaign tag. Cut the string into the maximum number of contiguous parts such that no character appears in more than one part. Return the array of part lengths in order.
constraints: events consists of lowercase English letters
hints: Har char ka last occurrence pre-compute kar.
Window walk kar: end = max(end, lastOccurrence[ch]). Jab i == end, ek part katt.
Empty string would return [], single char returns [1].

title: Valid Parenthesis String
description: Razorpay's webhook payload validator receives a string `s` containing only `'('`, `')'`, and `'*'`. The `*` may represent a `'('`, a `')'`, or an empty string. Return `true` if `s` can be made into a valid balanced parenthesis string, otherwise `false`.
hints: Track range [lo, hi] of possible open-counts as you scan.
Agar hi < 0 toh fail. lo ko 0 par clamp kar (negative possibility ka matlab hai us path ko discard).

title: Lemonade Change
description: A tea stall sells one cup at Rs 5. Customers pay with Rs 5, 10, or 20 notes (in queue order in `bills`). The stall starts with no cash. For each customer you must give exact change. Return `true` if all customers are served correctly, otherwise `false`.
hints: Sirf 5 aur 10 ke counts track karne padenge (20 ke wapas dene ka kaam hota nahi).
20 ke liye prefer karo: 1×10 + 1×5 (better than 3×5).
Greedy reason: 5 ka note flexible hai, isliye 10 pehle kharch karo.

title: Minimum Increment to Make Array Unique
description: Swiggy generated `orderIds` for the day but, due to a bug, some are duplicates. The fix: each duplicate may be incremented by +1 any number of times until all IDs are distinct. Return the minimum total number of increments needed.
hints: Sort kar de.
Adjacent traverse: agar arr[i] <= arr[i-1], usko arr[i-1] + 1 par push kar; cost += diff.
Empty array → 0.

title: Maximum Number of Events That Can Be Attended
description: BookMyShow lists `events`, where `events[i] = [startDay, endDay]` means event `i` runs every day from `startDay` to `endDay` inclusive. You can attend at most one event per day, and you may attend on any single day in `[startDay, endDay]`. Return the maximum number of events you can attend.

examples.explanation: Day 1 → event 0, Day 2 → event 1, Day 3 → event 2.

hints: Day-by-day simulate kar. Min-heap of endDays of currently-available events.
Har din: jo events aaj start hue add kar, jinka endDay aaj se chhota tha unhe pop kar.
Heap me sabse jaldi khatam hone wala event aaj attend kar.

title: Two City Scheduling
description: TCS is flying `2n` candidates to two interview cities, exactly `n` to City A and `n` to City B. `costs[i] = [costA, costB]` is the flight cost for candidate `i` to each city. Return the minimum total cost to fly exactly `n` candidates to A and `n` to B.
hints: Pretend everyone goes to A, total = sum of costA. Sending candidate i to B instead changes cost by (costB - costA).
Pick n candidates with smallest (costB - costA) to switch — sort by that diff.
Equivalent statement: sort by costA - costB; first n go to A.

title: Boats to Save People
description: Rapido is helping with a flood evacuation. Each boat has the same `limit` weight capacity and can carry at most two people. Given `weights[i]` per person, return the minimum number of boats needed (assume each weight is at most the limit).
hints: Sort kar, do pointers — heaviest aur lightest.
Agar dono fit ho jaayein, dono leke jao; warna heaviest akela.
Single person → 1 boat.

title: Minimum Cost to Hire K Workers
description: Ola needs to hire exactly `k` drivers from a pool of `n` candidates, where candidate `i` has `quality[i]` and an expected `wage[i]`. Pay rules: every hired driver in a group must be paid at least their wage; within the group, pays must be proportional to quality. Return the minimum total wage bill, accurate to 1e-5.

examples.explanation: Hire workers 0 and 2 with rate 7 → pay 70 + 35 = 105.

hints: Group ka 'rate' = max over members of (wage[i]/quality[i]).
Total = rate * (sum of qualities). So pick a candidate as 'captain' (sets rate), then add k-1 cheapest qualities among those with rate <= captain.
Sort by ratio; iterate, maintain max-heap of qualities of size k.

title: Task Scheduler
description: Zomato dispatcher must run a list of `tasks` (each task labeled by an uppercase letter). Identical tasks must be separated by at least `n` time units (idle if needed). Return the minimum total time units to finish all tasks.

examples.explanation: A -> B -> idle -> A -> B -> idle -> A -> B.

constraints: tasks[i] is an uppercase English letter
hints: Maximum frequency = f. (f - 1) windows ka length n+1, plus tail of all tasks tied at f.
Formula: max(tasks.length, (f-1)*(n+1) + numWithMaxFreq).
n = 0 → just tasks.length.

title: Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock II
description: Groww shows you the daily closing prices of a stock as `prices`. You may buy and sell as often as you want, but you must sell before you buy again (no shorting, no holding multiple lots). Return the maximum total profit.
hints: Har up-day ka diff jodte jao.
Equivalent: sum of max(0, prices[i] - prices[i-1]).
Single price → 0.

title: Largest Number
description: PhonePe gives you a list of non-negative integers `nums`. Concatenate them in some order to form the largest possible number, and return it as a string (without leading zeros, except a single '0' if the answer is zero).
hints: Sort by custom comparator: a vs b → compare strings (a+b) vs (b+a).
Edge case: leading zeros (e.g., [0,0] → '0').
Strings ka concatenation comparison hi correct order deta hai.

title: Bag of Tokens
description:
In a Dream11 mini-game you have an initial `power` value, a `score` of 0, and a multiset `tokens` of token values. Two operations on a token:
1. Play face up (cost: token's value in power; gain: +1 score). Requires `power >= token`.
2. Play face down (cost: -1 score; gain: token's value added to power). Requires `score >= 1`.
Each token is used at most once. Return the maximum achievable score.
hints: Sort tokens. Two pointers (l, r).
Agar power kaafi hai sabse chhota khelne ke liye → up, l++, score++.
Else agar score >=1 aur tokens bachhe hain → biggest down, r--, score--, power += big.

title: Non-overlapping Intervals
description: Myntra has booked `slots` for promotional banners as intervals `[start, end)`. Two slots overlap if their open ranges intersect. Return the minimum number of slots to remove so the remaining set is pairwise non-overlapping.
hints: Greedy interval scheduling: sort by end time.
Maintain currentEnd; agar slot.start >= currentEnd, keep it; warna remove count++.
Single interval → 0.

title: Minimum Number of Platforms
description: Uber's airport pickup zone has cabs arriving and departing throughout the day. Given two arrays `arrivals` and `departures` (same length, in HHMM integer format) for one day, return the minimum number of bays needed so that no cab is ever waiting outside. A cab arriving at the same minute another departs counts as overlap (needs a separate bay).
hints: Sort both arrays separately.
Two pointers: agar next arrival <= next departure, ek aur bay chahiye; warna ek free ho gayi.
Empty input → 0.

title: Candy
description: Amazon distributes Diwali candies to warehouse workers standing in a line. Each worker has a `rating[i]`. Rules: every worker gets at least 1 candy, and any worker with a strictly higher rating than an adjacent neighbor must get more candies than that neighbor. Return the minimum total candies needed.
hints: Two passes: left-to-right (left[i] = left[i-1]+1 if rating bigger else 1).
Right-to-left (right[i] = right[i+1]+1 if bigger else 1).
candy[i] = max(left[i], right[i]); sum nikaal.
